title: elaeocarpus calomala var. villosiusculus
intro: Elaeocarpus calomala var. villosiusculus, commonly known as the Tawau Oak, is a species of flowering plant in the Elaeocarpaceae family. It has been traditionally used in Southeast Asian medicine, where it is valued for its health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The therapeutic actions of the Tawau Oak include analgesic, anti-edematous, and antioxidant effects, which may help alleviate pain, reduce swelling, and protect against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other compounds with potential therapeutic activity.
benefits: Elaeocarpus calomala var. villosiusculus has many benefits, such as its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of arthritis, reduce stress, and even prevent certain types of cancer. This plant has also been found to have antimicrobial and antifungal properties, making it effective in treating wounds, skin infections, and respiratory issues like bronchitis and pneumonia. The leaves of Elaeocarpus calomala var. villosiusculus have been used to treat digestive problems like diarrhea and constipation, while its extracts have been shown to have anti-diabetic properties, helping to regulate blood sugar levels. By incorporating Elaeocarpus calomala var. villosiusculus into one's lifestyle, individuals can experience improved overall health, increased energy, and a reduced risk of chronic diseases, ultimately leading to a better quality of life.

These bioactive compounds can help alleviate symptoms of various diseases, such as reducing the risk of cardiovascular disease by inhibiting platelet aggregation and improving lipid profiles. parts: Elaeocarpus calomala var. villosiusculus has many medicinal parts, such as the leaves, flowers, and fruits, which are used in traditional medicine. The leaves of Elaeocarpus calomala var. villosiusculus contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The flowers of this plant contain saponins and terpenoids, which may have antimicrobial and antifungal activities. The fruits of Elaeocarpus calomala var. villosiusculus contain alkaloids and glycosides, which may have analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects.
side_effects: Elaeocarpus calomala var. villosiusculus can have side effects if used improperly, such as gastrointestinal issues, including nausea and diarrhea, due to its high toxicity levels. Additionally, consuming large quantities of this plant can lead to cardiovascular problems, including irregular heartbeats and decreased blood pressure. In severe cases, improper use of Elaeocarpus calomala var. villosiusculus can cause respiratory failure and even death.
